name: Abomination
content:
Abomination is a species encountered in wandering groups in Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, and Forgotten Lab.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Cloth Abomination 		Death Extra Padding: At the start of battle, this creature gains a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 200% of its highest stat. 42 22 12 17 17 Obtain Abomination Plush and bother it repeatedly 1

Ritual Abomination 		Death Turn to Grey: After this creature is Attacked, enemies are afflicted with Vulnerable. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 10

Abomination Brute 		Death Flesh Rot: After an enemy is afflicted with either Weak or Vulnerable, this creature afflicts them with whichever of these debuffs the target doesn't already have. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 15

Flesh Abomination 		Death Wretchedness: Your creatures' attacks against enemy creatures with Vulnerable cannot be Dodged and have a 50% greater chance to deal critical damage. This trait does not stack. 36 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 30

Abomination Volatile 		Death Quell: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Vulnerable, it deals 100% more damage. If this damage exceeds 35% of the target's Current Health, the target dies. 40 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 45

Gore Abomination 		Death Manipulate Fear: Enemy creatures with Weak have 50% less Attack and cannot deal critical damage. This trait does not stack. 36 23 13 18 18 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 60

Wandering Abomination 		Death Sin and Sentence: After this creature Attacks an enemy with both Weak and Vulnerable, it has a 50% chance to kill the target. 39 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 75

Bile Abomination 		Death Frailty: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Vulnerable, it deals 100% more damage, recovers Health equal to 100% of the damage dealt, and removes this debuff. 42 22 12 17 17 Path of the Damned, Titan's Wound, Forgotten Lab 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Abomination: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Abominations, enemies always have Weak and Vulnerable, and take 50% more damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

On a Master of Abominations team, Ritual Abomination and Abomination Brute are made redundant as there is no longer a need to apply Weak and/or Vulnerable. While the  Defiler specialization has traits that enhance Weak and Vulnerable, the funnier strategy is to use  Trickster's Even the Odds, a start-of-battle instant-kill effect, to cause Wandering Abominations with Centaur Raider's Anticipation trait to fish for a turn-zero victory.

As of version 2.0, when an enemy in a boss fight is hit with an instant-kill effect, they will instead take damage equal to 20% of their Missing Health.

Trivia[edit]

This species is most likely based on Frankenstein's Monster, an undead sewn together from body parts of other people.

name: Alemental
content:
Alemental is a species normally not encountered in Realms (only if you happen upon Alemental Master). You can get their Mana and trait materials by buying them from Gambling Dwarves.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Grenale 		Sorcery Slosh: After your creatures manually Cast a spell, a random enemy is afflicted with a random debuff. This trait does not stack. 33 18 18 18 18 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Winegel 		Life Hangover Cure: After your creatures heal an enemy, it has a 3% chance to be killed for each debuff it has. This trait does not stack. 34 19 19 19 19 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Lagergoyle 		Chaos Drinking Game: At the start of each enemy's turn, they have a 5% chance to be killed. This trait does not stack. 33 18 18 18 18 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Vulperry 		Nature Bob and Weave: Your creatures have an independent 25% chance to Dodge for each buff the enemy has. This trait does not stack. 33 18 18 18 18 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Scotchpion 		Chaos Drunken Rage: At the start of each enemy's turn, they have a 5% chance to attack one of their allies for each debuff they have. This trait does not stack. 33 18 18 20 20 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Ryetrap 		Death Alcoholism: After an enemy is afflicted with a debuff, they have a 10% chance to be sent to the bottom of the Timeline. This trait does not stack. 31 19 19 19 19 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Spirits 		Life Numbed Pain: When your creatures would take damage from an attack or spell, they have a 5% chance to take 0 damage for each debuff they have. This trait does not stack. 34 19 19 19 19 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Vermowl 		Sorcery False Epiphany: Enemies' spells cost 1 more Charge for every 2 debuffs they have. This trait does not stack. 33 18 18 18 18 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Woodka 		Nature Drunken Brawler: After your creatures Dodge an attack, they counter-attack for 40% normal damage. This trait does not stack. 32 17 20 20 20 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Whiskull 		Death Hangover: At the start of each enemy's turn, this creature has a 35% chance to Cast a random Booze Spell. This trait does not stack. 31 19 19 19 19 Gambling Dwarves Shop 37

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Alemental: 

At the start of battle, your  Alementals gain 2 random Booze Spells for each  Alemental fighting on your side. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Lost Amaranth 		Life Soulstone Splinter: After your creatures are Attacked, they gain 20% Defense. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 26 16 Eternity's End (Spires) 45

Enclave Amaranth 		Life Memento Mori: After this creature is Attacked, the enemy takes damage equal to 75% of this creature's Defense. 43 15 15 25 15 Eternity's End (Spires) 60

Amaranthine 		Life Incandescence: At the start of battle, this creature gains Maximum Health equal to 100% of its Defense. 39 15 15 25 15 Eternity's End (Spires) 75

Radiant Amaranth 		Life Crystallization: After this creature Casts a spell, your creatures gain 15% Defense. 39 15 15 25 15 Eternity's End (Spires) 90

Solar Amaranth 		Life Solar Flare: After this creature is Attacked while it is Defending or Provoking, it Casts Stellar Flare. This spell deals 50% more damage. 40 15 15 25 15 Chaos Guild 415

At the start of battle, your  Amaranths share 15% of their Defense with each other. This trait does not stack.

Siegemaster will interact nicely with the defense these things gain.

Trivia[edit]

Amaranth is a real flower, and a symbol of immortality.

Amphisbaena Chef 		Life Multicast: Your creatures Cast spells 1 additional time, but their spells are 30% less potent. This trait does not stack. 32 17 17 17 22 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell, Amalgam Gardens 40

Amphisbaena Gardener 		Life Unrealized Potential: After your creatures gain stats, they gain these stats an additional time, but they gain 50% fewer stats. This trait does not stack. 32 17 17 17 22 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell, Amalgam Gardens 45

Amphisbaena Inspector 		Life Unbidden Miracle: After your creatures are healed, they are healed an additional time, but they receive 50% less healing from all sources. This trait does not stack. 33 18 17 17 22 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell, Amalgam Gardens 60

Amphisbaena Prospector 		Life Careful What You Wish For: After your creatures gain a buff, they gain an additional random buff, but their buffs only last for 1 turn. This trait does not stack. 29 17 17 17 22 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell, Amalgam Gardens 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Amphisbaena: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Amphisbaenas, their innate traits' repsective effects activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Amphisbaena ("goes both ways" in greek) is a mythological, ant-eating serpent with a head at each end that spawned from the blood of Medusa.

Trivia[edit]

Angels in mythology of Abrahamic religions are usually benevolent beings that serve a higher divine. The concept is likely derived from Zoroastrian Yazatas.

name: Apis
content:
Apis is a species encountered in wandering groups in The Barrens, Overgrown Temple.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Apis Charger 		Life Threshold: After this creature Attacks, it afflicts the target with Scorned and Disarmed. 32 21 11 21 21 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 46

Apis Guardian 		Life Grit: At the start of battle, this creature's Attack is added to its Health and Defense, then its Attack is set to its minimum value. 26 21 11 21 21 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 46

Apis Defender 		Life Vigilance: This creature intercepts attacks on its allies. 28 18 13 23 23 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 46

Apis Endurer 		Life Long Live: This creature takes 2% less damage from attacks (up to 80%) for every 1% Health it is missing. 33 23 13 20 20 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 46

Apis Majesty 		Life Necessitarianism: At the start of battle, this creature gains 100% Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. This creature deals 70% less damage. 32 22 12 19 19 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 60

Apis Protector 		Life Righteous Concession: This creature and the creatures adjacent to it are resistant to debuffs. 31 21 11 21 21 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 75

Apis Warrior 		Life Crunch: This creature's attacks deal 75% more damage and afflict the target with Vulnerable. 27 22 12 22 22 The Barrens, Overgrown Temple 90

Apis Adolescent 		Life Primed And Ready: After an enemy Casts a spell on all your creatures, this creature Attacks it for 300% normal damage. 26 21 11 21 21 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Apis: 

At the start of battle, your  Apises gain the Long Live trait.

Strategy[edit]

Apis Defender is an awesome creature: normally ensuring a creature would intercept attacks would require acting first and Provoking, but Defender does it for free!

Also Apis Protector is an awesomely versatile creature: oneself and adjecent allies gain debuff resistance without Any Conditions, so your creatures get enough stabiliy agsinst some strongly restrictive defuffs; Frozen, Silenced, Snared, and more.

The other Apises have high potentialities as Attacker or Tunk, But let's be careful that some Apises are stats-changing or decresing dealing damages with their own traits.

Trivia[edit]

Apis is a deified bull in ancient Egyptian mythology.

name: Apocalypse
content:
This page is about the race of creatures. For other uses, see Apocalypse (spell).

 Apocalypse is a race encountered by interacting with Apocalypse Nests found in Great Pandemonium.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Aaxer Apocalypse 		Chaos Urh Harbinger: Your creatures have 15% more Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed for each turn they've taken in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 37 15 15 20 20 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 22

Agor Apocalypse 		Chaos Urdox Harbinger: Your creatures deal 50% more critical damage for each turn they've taken in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 40 15 15 20 20 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 22

Gorum Apocalypse 		Chaos Quahn Harbinger: Your creatures have a 25% chance to prevent debuffs for each turn they've taken in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 38 16 16 21 21 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 30

Inox Apocalypse 		Chaos Naxor Harbinger: Your creatures deal 40% more damage for each turn they've taken in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 37 15 15 20 20 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 45

Zarox Apocalypse 		Chaos Mungus Harbinger: Your creatures take 10% less damage for each turn they've taken in the current battle. Maximum of 90% damage reduction. This trait does not stack. 37 15 15 20 20 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 60

Corox Apocalypse 		Chaos Culmination: Your creatures' spells deal 5% more damage for each spell all your creatures have manually Cast in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 37 15 15 20 20 Great Pandemonium (Eggs) 75

Zzyia Apocalypse 		Chaos Phase Out: Your creatures have a 5% chance (up to 50%) to avoid damage for each turn they've taken in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 32 18 18 18 18 Chaos Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Apocalypse: 

Your  Apocalypses act as if they take 2 turns at once. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Quite obvious synergy with Reaver. Upon making it to Gate of the Gods, Anneltha or Lister will speed the snowball along.

Trivia[edit]

"Apocalypse" means "revelation" in ancient greek; originally (in 6th century BC, in jewish Babylonian exile) apocalypticism was mysticism based on visions (rather than god talking to people), but due to many of them being about the end of the world, word "apocalypse" currently means "end of the world".

name: Arachnalisk
content:
Arachnalisks are a race encountered by interacting with Spider Eggs in the Arachnid Nest. Arachnalisks work to prevent enemies from taking a turn until their Poisoned debuff is strong enough to kill them.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Canopy Arachnalisk 		Nature Here to Die: After an enemy is afflicted with either Bleeding or Poisoned, this creature afflicts them with whichever of these debuffs the target doesn't already have. 22 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Dragonlair Arachnalisk 		Nature Numbing Venom: After an enemy takes damage from Poisoned, they have a 50% chance to be afflicted with Snared. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Spitting Arachnalisk 		Nature Spit Poison: After this creature is Attacked, it afflicts enemies with Poisoned. If enemies already have this debuff, its potency is increased by 30% instead. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 34

Goliath Arachnalisk 		Nature Entrapment: After this creature is Attacked, it afflicts the enemy with Snared. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 45

Woolly Arachnalisk 		Nature Arachnophobia: Enemies with Snared take 100% more damage from their Poisoned debuffs. When they break free, they take damage equal to 5 turns of their Poisoned debuffs. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 60

Ancient Arachnalisk 		Nature Consume: When this creature Attacks an enemy with Snared, it deals 150% more damage, recovers Health equal to 100% of the damage dealt, and removes this debuff. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 75

Creeping Arachnalisk 		Nature Hopelessness: Enemies with Snared take 50% more damage. This trait does not stack. 28 23 23 13 18 Arachnid Nest (Eggs) 90

Razorweb Arachnalisk 		Nature Jagged Edges: At the start of each creature's turn, enemies with Snared take damage equal to 10% of this creature's Speed. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Arachnalisk: 

Enemies take 20% more damage from Poisoned for each  Arachnalisk fighting on your side. This trait does not stack.

Note: According to The Great Big List of Siralim Ultimate Formulas and Errata, Master of Arachnalisks "literally does NOTHING," but (as of 10/29/25) it hasn't yet been updated with changes from version 2.0 forward.

Strategy[edit]

Arachnalisks pretend that their niche is to drive a  Sorcerer team forward using a Poisoned-Snared debuff combo. Their actual niche is disappointing the player, and it goes further than their defective Mastery trait. When Dragonlair Arachnalisk's Numbing Venom applies Snared, the Snared creature does not get stuck at the bottom of the timeline until it finishes its turn. Spitting Arachnalisk and Goliath Arachnalisk asking to be Attacked while having base 13 Defense is an unfunny joke; they are at real risk of self-destruction when they attempt to use their traits, even when they cast Sabotage on their own teammates.

Outside their niche, the spiders aren't all that bad. A  Hell Knight using Here to Die and Flamehook Pilwiz's Kindling can apply Poisoned to all six enemies on turn zero, giving them a second source of Attack-based indirect damage. Their below-average Defense increases the damage dealt by the  Toxicologist Specialization's Prognosis perk, and Woolly Arachnalisk's new-and-improved Arachnophobia adds an additional way to proc Poison damage on an enemy.

name: Automaton
content:
Automaton is a species encountered by interacting with Inactive Automatons in Forgotten Lab．


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

name: Avatar
content:
Gate of the Gods content

This page or section contains information about things that will be encountered at Realm Depth 240 and deeper. Spoiler alert!

 Avatars are Gods of Rodia incarnated as creatures. You can obtain their mana by challenging them in the Gate of the Gods, and winning.

After you defeat a god, you will obtain some of its Mana (10% per difficulty, so 1 win at difficulty 10 will give 100% - enough to summon). After you acquire 100% Mana, you can summon that god's Avatar to join your party just like a normal creature. You can also use your Mana to upgrade your existing Avatar creatures at the Godforge (you will also need 100 Emblems to do that, though).

Each Avatar has a powerful trait along with an exclusive Ultimate Spell. Ultimate Spells are extremely powerful spells that have only 1 Charge, but this Charge regenerates after each battle (because they are ethereal).

You can only have 1 Avatar in your party at a time (unless you're Fanatic who can have up to 3).


Creature Class Trait Health Attack Intelligence Defense Speed Ultimate Spell
4080 Chaos Robotic Armor: Your creatures are resistant to debuffs. After your creatures are afflicted with a debuff, they have a 50% chance to gain Immune. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Heat Beam: Target is killed. If it cannot be killed, it takes a devastating amount of damage 3 times. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Aeolian Sorcery Periphery: Your creatures always Dodge attacks and deal critical damage, but take 50% more damage. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Eagle Claws: Caster Attacks each enemy 2 times, and your creatures gain 10% Speed for each attack. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Alexandria Death Boundless Creativity: After a creature gains or loses stats, its allies gain or lose 15% of those stats as well. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Infinite Blades: Enemies take a large amount of damage 5 times. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Anneltha Nature Grandiose: At the end of your creatures' turns, their turn counter increases by 3 additional turns. In addition, your creatures act as if they have 3 more buffs, debuffs, and minions than they actually have. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Tectonic Upheaval: Enemies take a massive amount of damage, lose a massive amount of Defense and Speed, and are afflicted with Snared. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Apocranox Nature Trick Shot: After this creature Attacks, it Casts 3 of its equipped Arrow spells. 42 17 17 17 17 Rain of Arrows: Enemies take a small amount of damage and the Caster Casts 5 random Arrow spells. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Ariamaki Nature Twilight Zone: Enemies deal damage with attacks and spells using their lowest stat, rather than their Attack or Intelligence. In addition, enemies take damage from attacks and spells based on their lowest stat, rather than Defense. 38 18 18 18 18 Grandeur: Your creatures gain a massive amount of stats and their Ultimate Spell Gems recover 100% Charges. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Aurum Sorcery Pandora: Your creatures can equip all Spell Gems, regardless of their class. After your creatures manually Cast a spell, they also Cast a random spell from their Spell Gem list from each other class. 36 16 16 19 19 Gem Bomb: One of the Caster's Ethereal Spell Gems is Sealed. Then, enemies take damage equal to 5% of the Caster's highest stat for each Charge the Spell Gem has. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Azural Sorcery Wintermaul: At the start of this creature's turn, it Casts Snowstorm. After your creatures Attack a Frozen enemy, they Cast Shatter on it. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 Frost Hammer: Target takes a devastating amount of damage, is afflicted with Frozen, and is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Erebyss Death Avenged Sevenfold: After your creatures take damage from an attack or spell, they Cast a Scourge spell on the enemy. The type of Scourge spell is based on the enemy's class. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Dark Strike: Target takes a devastating amount of damage, and is afflicted with all debuffs. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Friden Death The Black: Enemies always have Poisoned, Blighted, Leeching, and Mending. 38 18 18 17 18 Ghostly Scimitar: Target takes a devastating amount of damage which also ignores 80% of the target's Defense. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Genaros Chaos Surge of Vitality: After your creatures Attack or are Attacked, they gain 20% Maximum Health. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Total Carnage: Enemies are afflicted with Bleeding and this creature Attacks each enemy. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Gonfurian Chaos Eraser: After your creatures Attack, they reduce the target's Maximum Health by the same amount of damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 17 18 Sunder: Enemies take a large amount of damage and their Defense and Speed are set to their minimum values. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Lister Life Perfect Imperfection: Your creatures deal 40% more damage and take 10% less damage for each turn they've taken in the current battle. At the start of battle, your creatures gain the Timewalk spell. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 17 17 Peace: Enemies' Attack or Intelligence (whichever is highest) is set to their minimum values. Then, afflict each enemy with Scorned or Silenced, depending on whether their Attack or Intelligence was unaffected. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Meraxis Nature Marigold: Your creatures' stats gained in battle are no longer subject to diminishing returns. 36 16 19 19 19 Good Fortune: Your creatures gain all buffs. This spell does not activate on-buff effects. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Mortem Death Blood Pact: Your creatures deal additional damage equal to 5% of all the damage your creatures have dealt in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 17 17 Bloodwave: Enemies take damage equal to 50% of the Maximum Health the Caster gained in the current battle. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Muse Life Better Faster Stronger: Your creatures' minions repeat their effects twice. If they don't have an effect that can be repeated, they are instead 100% more powerful. This trait does not affect Illusion. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Work of Art: Your creatures gain an army of minions. This spell does not activate on-minion effects. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Perdition Life Euthanasia: After this creature damages an enemy below 50% Health, it Casts Death Siphon on it. 38 18 18 17 18 Afterlife: Your creatures' on-resurrect and on-death effects activate. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Reclusa Death Speculation: When your creatures Attack or Cast a spell, they have a 100% chance to do so 1 additional time. This effect can occur repeatedly, but the chance decreases by a multiplicative 25% each time. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Balance In All Things: Your creatures are healed or resurrected with a devastating amount of Health, and enemies take a devastating amount of damage. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Regalis Nature Brothel: At the start of battle, your other creatures with the same class as this creature share their innate trait with the creatures adjacent to them. This trait does not stack. 37 17 17 20 20 Seduction: Each enemy gives 25% of their Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed to your creatures in the enemies' corresponding positions. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Shallan Life Fae Dust: Your creatures deal 5% more damage with attacks and spells and take 5% less damage (up to 90%) for each time they've Attacked or Cast a spell in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Fae Fire: Enemies lose a devastating amount of all stats, and their buffs and minions are removed. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Surathli Life Prayer Position: At the end of your creatures' turns, they Defend. Your creatures take 90% less damage while they're Defending. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Holy Strike: Target takes a devastating amount of damage, and your creatures gain a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 100% of the damage dealt. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
T'mere M'rgo Sorcery Gemcutter: Your creatures' Ultimate Spell Gems cannot be sealed and do not consume Charges. Your creatures are immune to Silenced. 38 18 18 18 18 Quietus: The first time this Spell Gem is Cast, your creatures gain a squad of minions. The second time this Spell Gem is Cast, enemies take a massive amount of damage plus 15% more damage for each minion each of your creatures have, and then their minions are removed. Then, this spell is reset. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Tartarith Chaos The End: At the end of each enemy's turn, this creature deals damage to it equal to 40% of this creature's highest stat. This trait does not stack. 36 18 18 18 18 Butcher: Target takes a devastating amount of damage. In addition, your creatures gain Attack, Intelligence, Defense and Speed equal to 30% of the damage dealt. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Tenebris Sorcery Lingering Essence: This creature takes 18% less damage for each of its living allies. This trait does not work if another creature has this trait. 38 18 18 18 18 Hungering Void: Enemies take a moderate amount of damage, plus 30% for each time this spell has been Cast in the current battle. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Torun Nature I HATE YOU !@#$ING ALL: AT THE END OF YOUR CREATURES' TURNS, THEY'LL SAY A LOT OF !@#$ED UP !@#$ TO THE ENEMIES. SINCE YOUR ENEMIES HAVE THE EMOTIONAL FORTITUDE OF A PIKACHU, THEY'LL CRY LIKE A BUNCH OF !@#$%ES AND DEAL 90% LESS DAMAGE. THIS TRAIT DOES NOT STACK, WHATEVER THE !@#$ THAT MEANS. 36 16 16 19 19 Avalanche: Enemies take a devastating amount of damage and are sent to the bottom of the Timeline. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Venedon Chaos Soul Reaver: After this creature damages an enemy, it steals their buffs and minions and gives them its debuffs. In addition, your creatures' buffs and minions last forever, and enemies' debuffs last forever. 42 17 17 17 17 Shootout: Target takes a massive amount of damage. This spell has a 100% chance to deal critical damage and regenerates 1 Charge after an enemy is killed. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Vertraag Death Lune: After your creatures Cast a spell, they also Cast a random, powerful spell. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Comet: Enemies take a moderate amount of damage, multiplied by the number of turns the Caster has taken in the current battle. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Vulcanar Chaos Firelord: When your creatures Attack, they deal 20% more damage for each time they've attacked in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 38 18 18 18 18 Heat Wave: Enemies take a large amount of damage. In addition, enemies' Maximum Health is reduced by the amount of damage dealt. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.
Yseros Life The Truth: Your creatures' Artifacts' Nether Stones are 100% more powerful, and their Nether Stones' Spell Gems have a 100% chance to activate. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 Illusion: Your creatures gain an Illusion.
Zonte Sorcery Expanded Power: Your creatures' damaging spells that have 'small' 'moderate' or 'large' potency now have 'massive' potency. 38 18 18 18 18 Unstable Anomaly: Enemies take a small amount of damage multiplied by the number of times your creatures have Cast a spell in the current battle. This spell is an Ultimate Spell.

Avatars have no Mastery trait.

Avatars can be fused, but cannot be the secondary fusion.

Avatars can be upgraded at the Godforge for permanent boosts, such as more base stats, strength against classes, etc.

Strategy[edit]

Avatars provide extremely powerful traits to your team. For some examples: Genaros will let Bloodmage scale ridiculously; Torun will allow Paladin to do more actions per turn; Surathli is universally great for tankiness of your team; Regalis opens a whole new can of funny builds; Anneltha powers up Reaver greatly.

With Godforge bonuses, Avatars can be used to supplement traits/perks that require high base stats.

Anointing Multitheism will allow you to benefit from Avatar traits using  Godspawn.

Anointing Army of Gods will allow you to have up to three Avatars on your team at once.

Trivia[edit]

Word "Avatar" comes from a Hindu word for a concept of divine descent of a deity from their divine abode to the world; a related word, "avatarana" is a term for an actor moving from stage wings to the stage.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

A banshee is a fairy spirit from Irish folklore.

name: Basilisk
content:
Basilisks are a race exclusive to the God Shop of Vulcanar in Great Pandemonium.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Ethereal Basilisk 		Chaos Sluice: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, it Casts 3 of its Spell Gems at random. 34 19 19 19 14 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 22

Dormant Basilisk 		Chaos Here Until Forever: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, it recovers 100% Health and gains Rebirth. 34 19 19 19 14 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 22

Lurking Basilisk 		Chaos Run Free: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, it Attacks each enemy. 35 20 20 20 15 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 8) 22

Crashing Basilisk 		Chaos Awakening: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, each of your creatures Cast one of their Spell Gems at random. 36 21 21 16 16 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 22

Roaring Basilisk 		Chaos No Sudden Movement: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, it afflicts enemies with Snared and decreases their Speed by 30%. 32 20 20 20 15 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 22

Stomping Basilisk 		Chaos Sap: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, this creature Seals all the enemies' Spell Gems. 35 20 20 20 15 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 22

Spitting Basilisk 		Chaos Heated Ichor: If this creature does not take damage from attacks or spells between each of its turns, it deals damage to enemies equal to 50% of its Current Health. 35 20 20 20 15 Vulcanar God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 22

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Basilisk: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Basilisks, they have a 20% chance to avoid damage. In addition, their innate traits have a 25% chance to activate even if they took damage between their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

A creature's Basilisk trait(s) activate when it is sent to the top of the timeline, regardless of if it's able to take action before being displaced by another creature being sent to the top of the timeline. When a creature is sent to the top of the timeline, its turn counter raises, regardless of if it's able to take action before being displaced by another creature being sent to the top of the timeline, perhaps triggering its Basilisk trait in the process, perhaps while benefitting from  Apocalypse traits or  Reaver perks. The Timewalk spell is a good way to trigger Basilisk traits artificially.

Trivia[edit]

Bats are a real animal. Their legs don't look like that. Also, their knees are backwards from all other mammals. Ridiculous animals.

Strategy[edit]

Brownie Brute's trait does awesome damage, and doubly so for teams that wield Health offensively ( Giants, Bloodmage, etc).

Trivia[edit]

Brownies are house spirits from Scottish folklore.

name: Calumnier
content:
Calumnier is a species encountered in the Overgrown Temple in wandering groups.


The realm depth at which each  Calumnier first appears is currently unknown, and the values in the table below are a placeholder.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Calumnier Bete 		Death Spite Match: Enemies cannot gain this creature's Personality-decreased stats, and this creature Attacks enemies when this would happen. 32 25 20 15 18 Overgrown Temple 1

Calumnier Dandy 		Death Stolen Valor: Your creatures cannot lose extra traits and Spell Gems in battle. 32 25 20 20 23 Overgrown Temple 1

Calumnier Partycrasher 		Death Su Casa Es Mi Casa: This creature deals 200% more damage to enemies with the same Personalities that decrease the same stats as this creature's Personality. 32 25 20 15 18 Overgrown Temple 1

Calumnier Socialite 		Death Load-Bearing Makeup: Your creatures' Personality-increased stats cannot be decreased. 35 20 20 25 20 Overgrown Temple 1

If all the creatures in your party are  Calumniers, they do not have decreased stats from their Personalities.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Calumny[1] is the act of spreading malicious lies or gossip.

name: Carbuncle
content:
Carbuncle is a species encountered by interacting with in Horseshoes in Temple of Lies．


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Emerald Carbuncle 		Sorcery Paramount: After this creature takes damage, it has a 2% chance to Cast one of its spells for each spell that has been Cast by all creatures in the current battle. This trait can only activate once per turn. 35 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 28

Aquamarine Carbuncle 		Sorcery Thirst For Power: After your creatures Cast a spell, they have a 7% chance to gain a random buff for each spell that has been Cast by all creatures in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 35 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 28

Onyx Carbuncle 		Sorcery Magic Barrier: If your creatures have Cast a total of 12 or more spells in the current battle, your creatures are immune to debuffs. 38 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 30

Ruby Carbuncle 		Sorcery Annex: This creature has 5% more Intelligence for each spell that has been Cast by all creatures in the current battle. 35 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 45

Topaz Carbuncle 		Sorcery Absolute Power: This creature's attacks deal 5% more damage for each spell that has been Cast by all creatures in the current battle. 38 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 60

Sapphire Carbuncle 		Sorcery Nirvana: If your creatures have Cast a total of 7 or more spells in the current battle, this creature's spells are 50% more potent and no longer consume Charges. 31 13 23 13 23 Temple of Lies (Horseshoes) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Carbuncle: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Carbuncles, their innate traits grow stronger twice as fast. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Carbuncle is a creature from south american folklore - an animal with a red precious stone in its head. Its name originates from latin "carbunculus" - "little coal", a name for red gemstones - ruby and garnet.

Appearance of Siralim carbuncles is likely inspired by carbuncles from Final Fantasy XIV.

name: Cataclysm
content:
Cataclysm is a species encountered in the Land of Breath and Balance in wandering groups.

The realm depth at which each  Cataclysm first appears is currently unknown, and the values in the table below are a placeholder.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Volcanic Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Volcano: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Magma Shower. 40 25 20 20 15 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Terra Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Earth: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Richter 10. 45 20 15 28 12 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Storm Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Storm: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Raiden. 35 20 25 15 25 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Sky Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Sky: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast EF5 Twister. 38 20 25 20 17 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Pandemic Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Pandemic: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Cloud of Anguish. 35 15 28 20 22 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Nuclear Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of Humanity: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Nuclear Blast. 38 28 28 15 11 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Glacial Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Glacier: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Icicle Rain. 38 15 25 25 17 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Cosmic Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Cosmos: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Kilonova. 42 25 25 25 23 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Almighty Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Almighty: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Death and Destruction. 40 25 25 25 25 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Explosive Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Great War: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Grand Explosion. 35 28 20 15 22 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Oceanic Cataclysm 		Nature Wrath of the Ocean: At the end of each creature's turn, this creature has a 10% chance to Cast Mega Tsunami. 42 15 25 25 13 Land of Breath and Balance 1

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Cataclysm: 

If all of the creatures in your party are  Cataclysms, their traits have a 10% chance to activate at the start of each creature's turn as well. This trait does not stack.

Lore[edit]

Cataclysms are highly volatile, highly illegal weapons created by condensing several Spell Gems into an "eye" over the course of days. They unleash their spell uncontrollably, posing a constant danger to everyone nearby. While it is possible to safely contain a cataclysm, its spell-casting creates a buildup of pressure which will eventually burst its container if ignored. Sealed cataclysms are sometimes used as bombs.

Strategy[edit]

Chimera

Blessed Chimera 		Sorcery Raging Spirit: After your creatures are killed, they have a 35% chance to be resurrected with 50% Health and gain a random trait that belongs to their Fused creature's race. This trait does not stack. 37 17 17 20 20 T'Mere M'rgo God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 64

Undying Chimera 		Sorcery Appreciation For Ancestry: Your creatures deal 75% more damage and take 50% less damage from creatures that belong to their Fused creature's class. This trait does not stack. 33 16 16 21 21 T'Mere M'rgo God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 64

Valor Chimera 		Sorcery Valiant Heart: Your creatures Attack and Cast 1 additional time for each creature with the same secondary Fusion fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 21 21 T'Mere M'rgo God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 64

Wylder Chimera 		Sorcery Fairy Blood: At the start of battle, your creatures gain a random trait that belongs to their Fused creature's class. This trait does not stack. 35 15 15 20 20 T'Mere M'rgo God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 64

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Chimera: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Chimeras, they take 75% less damage from creatures that belong to their fused creature's class. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Chimera traits with stats tied to classes affect the following:

  • Life: Corresponds to the Health (HP) stat.
  • Death: Corresponds to the Defense stat.
  • Chaos: Corresponds to the Attack stat.
  • Sorcery: Corresponds to the Intelligence stat.
  • Nature: Corresponds to the Speed stat.

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Diabolic Horde: 

At the start of battle, your creatures' races are changed to  Diabolic Horde.

Strategy[edit]

Diabolic Horde thrive on headcount of their own kind, so Tribalist will find them an easy team to handle. Clamorous Cockatrice's Odd One will add some more "heads" to count. The mastery trait leaves a lot of wiggle room for builds taking advantage of Horde's traits.

Strategy[edit]

The race has very high Intelligence specialized spellcasting and spell gems, including on−Defend or on−Provoke effects. Djinns would help many Spell-based builds and Specializations, such as  Evoker,  Cabalist, and some options on  Doombringer, etc.

Trivia[edit]

Djinn is known as supernatural beings neither innately evil nor innately good in pre-Islamic Arabian religion and Islam.

name: Dryad
content:
Dryads are a race exclusive to the God Shop of Shallan in the Fae Lands.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Dryad Keeper 		Nature Surge: When your creatures gain a buff, they gain 15% Attack and Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 28 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 64

Dryad Naturalist 		Nature Extension: After this creature Defends, the duration of your creatures' buffs is extended by 2 turns, and your creatures' minions gain 1 stack. 28 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 64

Dryad Proliferator 		Nature Moonlight: Your creatures have 7% more Attack, Intelligence, and Defense for each buff they have. This trait does not stack. 29 17 17 22 22 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 8) 64

Dryad Raincaller 		Nature Soothing Rain: After this creature Defends, your creatures recover 20% Health for each buff they have. 32 17 17 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 64

Dryad Seductress 		Nature Green Eyes: After any creature gains a buff, this creature gains that buff as well. 31 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 64

Dryad Warden 		Nature Duplication: After this creature Provokes, your other creatures gain a copy of this creatures' buffs. 30 15 15 23 20 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 64

Dryad Vindicator 		Nature Absolution: After an enemy is afflicted with a debuff, they lose 15% Attack and Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 21 21 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 64

Dryad Sunbather 		Nature Oulala: Enemies that are Confused deal 150% more damage to each other with attacks. This trait does not stack. 28 16 16 21 21 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Dryad: 

Your  Dryads' buffs and minions last forever.

Strategy[edit]

While Dryads may be typecast in  Warden teams, sometimes  Cleric ones as well, the myriad of ways to apply Buffs and Debuffs allows nearly any build to benefit from at least one Dryad trait. Dryad Vindicator and Dolor Sin create a Stat Decrease-Debuff loop that, with the right statblock, can deal massive amounts of indirect damage via  Defiler's Unholy Night perk, or quickly neuter an enemy when combined with Leper Plaguespreader's innate trait. Once a  Necromancer gets access to Andrick's Tattered Garb during the postgame, Dryad Keeper's Surge can cause each of their creatures' offensive stats to skyrocket with few or no changes in strategy. Even when it isn't the main focus of a build, any Specialization that can rapidly apply Buffs and Debuffs, such as  Witch Doctor or  Brewmaster, can revel in Dryad traits, because having Buffs and having Stats are just plain good. The only limit to teambuilding with Dryads is how much time you're willing to spend in the Fae Lands.

Amphisbaena Vaccinator will help proc Absolution and loses its trait's downside with Defiler's perk Lingering Sickness (which became Annointable in 2.0!), while Amphisbaena Prospector will help proc Surge and loses its trait's downside with Master of Dryads. Pair the innate traits of Dryad Seductress and Ectoplasmic Slime for a great passive Buffing support.

name: Electropod
content:
Electropods are a race encountered in the Forgotten Lab and the Gambler's Hive in wandering groups. Like Vin Diesel, these electronic arthropods have a need for Speed and know the value of family.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Electropod Mantis 		Nature Networking: Your creatures' Electropod traits act as if your party contains 3 more Electropod creatures. This trait does not stack. 30 15 15 20 25 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Beetle 		Nature High Performance: Your Electropod creatures have 7% more Speed for each empty Spell Gem slot they have. Maximum of 8 Spell Gem slots per creature. This trait does not stack. 30 15 15 20 25 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Spider 		Nature Malware: At the start of each enemy's turn, this creature has a 20% chance to deal damage to them equal to 40% of its Speed. This trait does not stack. 30 15 15 20 25 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Ant 		Nature Thread Pool: Your Electropod creatures have 10% more Speed for each Electropod fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 28 16 16 21 26 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Fly 		Nature Infinite Scaling: At the start of battle, your Electropod creatures share 15% of their Speed with each other. This trait does not stack. 32 15 15 20 25 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 64

Electropod Scorpion 		Nature Spin Cycle: At the start of this creature's turn, it deals damage to a random enemy equal to 75% of its Speed. 33 18 18 17 22 Forgotten Lab, Gambler's Hive 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Electropod: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Electropod, they gain 50% more Speed from stat-boosting effects. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Electropods' Speed addiction gives them obvious synergy with the  Windrunner specialization, but their various traits promoting race-stacking makes them an excellent choice for  Tribalists as well. Whether gaining Speed on a Windrunner team or having Speed on a Tribalist team, using Electropods leads to the same end result: dealing game-breaking damage using Lead the Charge.

For a Tribalist team, double-down on the Specialization's horde mentality using  Cockatrice traits, Sphinx Elder's Pharaoh's Boon, Sphinx Ordainer's Blessing of Sands, and, eventually, Shackler's Signet's two imaginary Electropods. By pairing the Tribalist perk We Are Pack with Clawing Cockatrice's Outcast No Longer, it only takes one Electropod to run a Master of Electropods team. This is fantastic, since the traits of Draco Chimera and Underdweller Mischiefmaker incentivize using Electropods as the secondary fusions because the Nature class is associated with Speed.

Trivia[edit]

One of the species contibuted by an unknown backer.

name: Elementasaur
content:
The  Elementasaur race is encountered in the Cutthroat Jungle in wandering groups. Rather than remove or prevent debuffs, Elementasaurs thrive under specific debuffs!


The realm depth at which each  Elementasaur first appears is currently unknown, and the values in the table below are a placeholder.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Vinosaurus 		Nature Barbed Vines: This creature always has Snared. Your creatures with Snared intercept attacks and single-target spells on allies and take damage based on their Attack instead of Defense. This trait does not stack. 35 25 15 25 10 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Holyguanodon 		Nature Ignorance Is Bliss: This creature always has Mania and Mending. After your creatures take damage from Mania, a random enemy takes that damage as well. After your creatures are healed by Mending, a random enemy is healed for that amount as well. This trait does not stack. 35 20 25 20 10 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Darknonychus 		Nature Sleepwalking: This creature always has Sleeping. Your creatures with Sleeping Attack or Cast their top-most spell at the start of their turns. Your creatures with Sleeping have a 50% chance to afflict the target with Sleeping with attacks and spells. This trait does not stack. 32 25 20 15 18 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Triflaratops 		Nature Molten Spray: This creature always has Scorned. After your creatures with Scorned Attack, they deal damage to each enemy equal to 50% of the damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 35 25 15 20 15 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Thunder-Rex 		Nature Negative Charge: This creature always has Silenced. Your creatures' Lightning Storm acts as if they have 50% more Intelligence for each creature on your team afflicted with Silenced. This trait does not stack. 38 25 20 20 17 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Veliceoraptor 		Nature Frost Affinity: This creature always has Frozen. Your creatures with Frozen can still act and can Cast spells that interact with Frozen without consuming Charges. This trait does not stack. 30 20 25 15 25 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Brockiosaurus 		Nature Orthoclase: This creature always has Stone. Your creatures with Stone have 50% more Defense. Enemies cannot be resistant to Stone. This trait does not stack. 40 15 15 28 12 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Windactyl 		Nature Sandstorm: This creature always has Blind. When your creatures with Blind Cast a spell that targets more than one creature, it instead targets all creatures and has 100% more potency. This trait does not stack. 30 15 25 15 28 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Ancloudysaur 		Nature Cloudy Constitution: This creature always has Vulnerable. Your creatures with Vulnerable gain 50% more stats from all sources. This trait does not stack. 32 20 20 15 25 Cutthroat Jungle 1

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Elementasaur: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Elementasaurs, they are resistant to debuffs and at the start of battle, they share the debuffs referenced in their traits with each other. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Traits like Favored Masochist's Lust for Punishment, Masochist Angler's Under Stress, and Spirits' Numbed Pain were created for a time when "Always Have" debuffs weren't inflicted on the traitholder's side. On a Master of Elementasaurs team, having all three of the listed traits at the start of battle immediately grants 240% more sourced damage output, six dodge and critical damage rerolls, and a 30% chance to negate damage received.

While the above may sound appealing, using even one Elementasaur on a team expects you to remake your strategy in its image, while using six is a juggling act. Triflaratops and Thunder-Rex's traits have mutually-exclusive benefits and require your creatures to resist debuffs. Windactyl's Sandstorm, without any Shapeshifter traits active, is a recipe for friendly-fire - however, on a Master of Elementasaurs team, friendly-fire may be desirable, as Holyguanodon's Ignorance is Bliss requires opportunities to heal. Vinosaurus' Barbed Vines fares well enough supporting a non-Elememtasaur team, namely among  Manticores, but it only belongs on an Elementasaur team if you're seriously confident in your creatures' ability to proc Casts and/or Attacks.

Trivia[edit]

The  Elementasaur race was contributed by the backer "Playmikesux".

In addition, each creature of the species takes a real-world dinosaur for inspiration: Tyrannosaurus Rex, Triceratops, Velociraptor, Spinosaurus, Brachiosaurus, Pterodactyl, Ankylosaurus, Deinonychus, and Iguanodon, respectively.

name: Exotic
content:
Exotics are a "species" of unique creatures encountered in Otherworld Portals. They don't belong to any other species, and are therefore exotic.

Contents

  • 1 Otherworld Portals
  • 2 How do I get more Exotics?
  • 3 List
  • 4 Trivia

Otherworld Portals[edit]

Otherworld Portals are very rarely encountered in Realms. They look like rainbow gates that lead to a boss room, in which a creature group led by the  Exotic creature resides.

How do I get more Exotics?[edit]

In 2.0, you can buy all exotic creatures from the Nethermancer for 10,000 stardust. You acquire stardust by defeating exotics.

Otherwise, to get more Mana of an Exotic you already have, you will need Knowledge of them. You can do this in two ways.

  1. The first way is by accumulating Knowledge through drops, which is unpredictable. This is potentially faster in realms like the Arachnid Nest, Kingdom of Heretics and Overgrown Temple, all of which have increased Knowledge rewards for higher favor.
  2. A more reliable way is to use the Mirrorball and Mysterious orbs in the Azure Dream or Overgrown Temple, making sure you have the Exotic you wish to farm on your team. There is one orb per map, and each time you use it you will fight a copy of your own creatures.

Once you have Knowledge rank D, start a Hunt for the creature you need. The Hunt will elevate them to rank C or higher, and then you can use the Divination Candle to summon and defeat them for summoning.

Trivia[edit]

You can pay 200$ to add your own Exotic to the game. Or 1000$ for a whole new species.

buffs.name: Barrier
buffs.description: Barrier absorbs a certain amount of damage. After the maximum amount of damage is dealt, the buff is removed.

buffs.name: Invisible
buffs.description: Invisible creatures cannot be targeted by enemy attacks or spells. This buff is removed after the creature takes an action.

buffs.name: Leeching
buffs.description: Creatures with Leeching recover health equal to 25% of the damage they deal.

buffs.name: Mending
buffs.description: Creatures with Mending recover 25% health at the start of their turn.

buffs.name: Rebirth
buffs.description: Creatures with Rebirth are resurrected with 50% health when they die.

buffs.name: Shelled
buffs.description: Shelled negates the next incoming attack or spell.

buffs.name: Splashing
buffs.description: Creatures with Splashing deal 100% of their damage to enemies adjacent to the target they attack.

debuffs.name: Bleeding
debuffs.description: Creatures with Bleeding take damage equal to 30% of their current health at the start of each turn.

debuffs.name: Blighted
debuffs.description: Creatures with Blighted cannot be healed and take damage from healing effects equal to 50% of the effect.

debuffs.name: Blind
debuffs.description: Blind creatures cannot choose their targets when attacking or casting single-target spells.

debuffs.name: Bomb
debuffs.description: At the start of a creature's turn, its Bomb detonates, dealing damage equal to 80% of the afflictor's Speed and then is removed.

debuffs.name: Burning
debuffs.description: Creatures with Burning take damage based on the inflictor’s Intelligence each turn.

debuffs.name: Confused
debuffs.description: Confused creatures have a 50% chance to attack or cast harmful spells on their allies.

debuffs.name: Feared
debuffs.description: Feared creatures cannot gain stats.

debuffs.name: Frozen
debuffs.description: Frozen creatures cannot act until they thaw; chance to thaw increases each turn.

debuffs.name: Poisoned
debuffs.description: Creatures with Poisoned take damage based on the inflictor's Attack each turn.

debuffs.name: Sleeping
debuffs.description: Sleeping creatures cannot act until they take damage or the debuff expires.

debuffs.name: Snared
debuffs.description: Snared creatures are stuck at the bottom of the timeline until they break free (chance each turn).

debuffs.name: Stone
debuffs.description: Creatures with Stone cannot dodge or deal critical damage.

name: Fae
content:
Fae is a species encountered by interacting with Fairy Groups in Fae Lands.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Hydrangea Fae 		Life Crystal Lake: This creature Attacks and Casts spells 2 additional times, but can't gain buffs. 25 25 25 9 19 Fae Lands (Fairy Groups) 64

Polygala Fae 		Life True North: This creature's start-of-turn and end-of-turn effects activate 1 additional time, but it has 95% less stats (other than Health). 21 21 21 13 23 Fae Lands (Fairy Groups) 64

Rhododendron Fae 		Life The Great Beyond: This creature's automatically-Cast spells have a 40% chance to be considered manually Cast, but this creature cannot manually Cast spells. 24 24 24 9 19 Fae Lands (Fairy Groups) 64

Asclepias Fae 		Life Dead Butterflies: The first 3 times this creature is killed, it is resurrected with 100% Health. This creature takes 75% more damage and deals 50% less damage. 27 27 27 6 16 Fae Lands (Fairy Groups) 64

Roseaea Fae 		Life Outgrow: This creature's end-of-turn effects activate 1 additional time, but it has 50% less stats (other than Health). 26 26 24 9 19 Fae Lands (Fairy Groups) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Fae: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Fae, they have a 60% chance to avoid damage from spells. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

name: Familiar
content:
Familiar is a species available only via Zonte's emblem shop in Refuge of the Magi.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Elemental Familiar 		Sorcery Conversion: Your creatures' spells deal 15% more damage and 15% less healing for each  Familiar fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 33 18 28 13 13 Zonte God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 19

Arcane Familiar 		Sorcery All For One: Your creatures Cast spells 0.5 additional times (rounded down) for each  Familiar fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 28 16 26 16 16 Zonte God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 19

Mystical Familiar 		Sorcery Group Meditation: At the start of battle, your creatures' Spell Gems gain 1 Charge if you have at least 3  Familiars fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 31 16 26 16 16 Zonte God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 19

Palace Familiar 		Sorcery Potency: Your creatures' spells that increase or decrease stats are 25% more effective for each  Familiar fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 32 16 26 16 16 Zonte God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 19

Spectral Familiar 		Sorcery Higher Understanding: Your creatures' Spell Gems have a 10% chance to not consume Charges when they're Cast for each  Familiar fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 32 17 25 15 15 Zonte God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 19

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Familiar: 

Your  Familiars have 10% more Intelligence for each time your  Familiars have Cast a spell in the current battle. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

A good team for a spell-focused Tribalist.

buffs.name: Arcane
buffs.description: When an Arcane creature casts a spell, the spell does not consume any Charges. Then, this buff is removed.

buffs.name: Berserk
buffs.description: Berserk creatures deal 50% more damage.

buffs.name: Invisible

name: Forsaken
content:
Forsaken is a species purchased from Alexandria in Damarel. They are the female counterpart to  Ghouls.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Forsaken Bonescraper 		Death Whetted Bones: This creature starts battles with 100% more Attack and Defense, but loses 25% Attack and Defense at the end of its turn. 38 18 16 21 16 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 64

Forsaken Plaguebearer 		Death Anxiety: This creature's Spell Gems have 100% more potency, but are Sealed after they're Cast. 37 17 17 22 17 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 64

Forsaken Rotmongerer 		Death Stupify: This creature starts battles with 100% more Intelligence and Defense, but loses 25% Intelligence and Defense at the end of its turn. 36 16 16 22 16 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 8) 64

Forsaken Scourgewalker 		Death Curse of the Silent: This creature's Spell Gems don't lose Charges when it Casts spells, but it also starts battles with Silenced. 32 17 17 22 17 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 64

Forsaken Seacrawler 		Death Transmogrification: This creature starts battles with 5 random buffs, but cannot gain additional buffs. 36 16 16 23 18 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 64

Forsaken Spinewhipper 		Death Contradiction: This creature starts battles with 200% more Speed, but also starts battles at the bottom of the Timeline. 38 18 18 20 15 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 64

Forsaken Swampdweller 		Death Darkness Surrounding: This creature starts battles with 125% more Health, but takes damage equal to 15% of its Maximum Health at the start of its turn. 33 13 18 23 18 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 64

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Forsaken: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Forsaken, their innate traits have no downside.

Strategy[edit]

What are you doing with that forsaken? Gonna hit me? Better make it count. Better make it hurt. Better kill me in one turn, before they start to deflate.

debuffs.name: Silenced
debuffs.description: Silenced creatures cannot Cast spells; resistant creatures can still cast at -50% damage.

name: Gargantuan
content:
Gargantuan is a species encountered in wandering groups in Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, and Forbidden Depths.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Monstrous Gargantuan 		Nature Bide: When this creature Attacks, it deals additional damage equal to 100% of the damage it took since it last Attacked. 42 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 4

Sturdy Gargantuan 		Nature Defer Pain: When this creature takes damage, 90% of the damage is delayed until the start of its next turn. 42 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 15

Shackled Gargantuan 		Nature Come Hither: After this creature is Attacked, it Casts Crush Punch on the enemy. 39 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 30

Daunting Gargantuan 		Nature Furor: At the start of this creature's turn, it Attacks a random enemy for each creature that died since its last turn. Each attack deals 200% normal damage. 39 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 45

Fearsome Gargantuan 		Nature Charge: At the start of your creatures' turns, this creature has a 25% chance to Attack a random enemy. 42 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 60

Forest Gargantuan 		Nature Resprout: At the start of this creature's turn, it recovers Health equal to 100% of the damage it took since its last turn. 39 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 75

Gruesome Gargantuan 		Nature Scrutiny: At the start of this creature's turn, it Casts a random spell for each spell that the enemy creatures Cast since its last turn. 42 22 12 27 7 Frostbite Caverns, Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 90

Volcanic Gargantuan 		Nature Spontaneous Combustion: After this creature is Attacked, it Casts Volcano. 42 7 27 28 8 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Gargantuan: 

Your  Gargantuans have a 50% chance to avoid damage while they're Defending or Provoking. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Meaning of Gargantuan is extremely large, gigantic, or colossal; derived from François Rabelais's The Inestimable Life of Gargantua.

name: Giant
content:
Giant is a species encountered in wandering groups in Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Wall Giant 		Nature Hostile Vitality: When this creature Attacks, it deals additional damage equal to 35% of its Current Health. 42 22 12 22 12 Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 30

Tower Giant 		Nature Wreck: When this creature Attacks, its damage is based on its Current Health instead of Attack. 43 22 12 22 12 Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 45

Cosmic Giant 		Nature Admire the Gods: When this creature takes damage, and the damage exceeds 25% of its Maximum Health, reduce the damage to 25% of its Maximum Health. The amount of damage prevented cannot exceed 200% of this creature's Maximum Health. 40 22 12 22 12 Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 60

Colossal Giant 		Nature Strength of the World: At the start of battle, this creature's Defense is added to its Maximum Health, then its Defense is set to its minimum value. In addition, this creature takes 50% less damage. 40 20 13 23 13 Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 75

Giant Mangler 		Nature Boastful Protector: While this creature is Provoking, it has a 25% chance to avoid damage. 43 23 13 18 13 Titan's Wound, Forbidden Depths 90

War Giant 		Chaos Reforged: After your creatures are resurrected, they gain 50% Maximum Health. This trait does not stack. 42 22 12 22 12 Chaos Guild 415

Jolly Old Giant 		Nature Ho Ho Oh, !@#$: Creatures cannot gain stats until after their first turn. 39 22 12 22 12 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Giant: 

Your  Giants start battles with 30% more Health for each  Giant fighting on your side. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Double down on them as Bloodmage, or use them even without - they are strong even without specialization. Well-equipped Mystical Giant can wipe out enemies at the start of battle for a Hell Knight with Firestorm.

Trivia[edit]

name: Godspawn
content:
Godspawn is a special "species". Godspawn do not roam the Realms, but can only be obtained one at a time via doing a special Project that require purchasing their Heart from their "parent" God.

Diamant 		Sorcery Skyttels: At the start of battle, this creature gains copies of your other creatures' non-Ethereal Spell Gems. This trait does not work on creatures that also have this trait. 22 20 25 20 15 Aurum God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 28

Ugat 		Nature Karma: After this creature Attacks or is damaged by an attack or spell, it gains 50% of its lowest stat. 32 17 17 20 20 Torun God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 31

Alakadan 		Nature Hopelessly Hopeful: After this creature manually Casts a spell, it also Casts a random Avatar spell. 33 18 18 18 18 Regalis God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 34

Walken 		Death Closure: At the end of this creature's turn, it gains 35% Current and Maximum Health and Provokes. 34 19 19 19 19 Vertraag God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 37

Mumu 		Death Revolt: After this creature Attacks or takes damage from an attack or spell, it afflicts the enemy with a random debuff. 37 22 17 17 17 Friden God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 40

Dikya 		Life Eve: Your creatures' spells are 100% more potent, but only have 1 Charge. This trait does not stack. 34 19 18 18 18 Lister God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 43

Laglor 		Life Nostalgia: At the start of your creatures' turns, they Cast a random spell that belongs to their class. This trait does not stack. 34 19 19 19 19 Yseros God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 46

Kalasag 		Sorcery Rescue Ride: At the end of this creature's turn, it resurrects a random ally with 100% Health and gives it Shelled. 33 18 18 18 18 Aeolian God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 49

Darksulker 		Sorcery From Death to Destiny: This creature has additional stats equal to 50% of the stats of your dead creatures. This trait does not work with creatures who also have this trait. 33 18 18 18 18 Tenebris God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 52

The Lost 		Chaos Beyond Oblivion: This creature receives all effects that require it to be adjacent to your other creatures. This trait does not stack. 36 21 16 16 16 Tartarith God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 55

Iconus 		Chaos Silence Speaks: At the start of battle, this creature gains two of your other creatures' innate traits. 33 18 18 18 18 Gonfurian God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 58

Tenbran 		Life To Move On Is To Grow: This creature's attacks deal 100% more damage if it Cast a spell last turn. This creature's spells deal 100% more damage if it Attacked last turn. 29 22 22 17 17 Perdition God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 61

8004 		Chaos Something From Nothing: Your creatures deal 5% more damage with attacks and spells, and take 1% less damage (up to 80%) for each time this creature has taken damage in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 4080 God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Emus 		Life Fear of Success: This creature's attacks deal 75% more damage and afflict the target with Inverted. 42 17 17 17 17 Muse God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Rengas 		Chaos Rodian Aftermath: This creature deals 30% more damage with attacks and spells for each time each creature has died in the current battle. 42 17 17 17 17 Genaros God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Saurel 		Death Syndicate: At the start of your Godspawn creatures' third turns, they transform into their corresponding Avatar. 40 17 17 17 17 Reclusa God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Nalas 		Life Oathbreaker: Your creatures gain 100% more benefit from stat-increasing Realm Boosts. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 Shallan God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Thana 		Nature Negative Noise: While this creature is at 100% Health, it has a 90% chance to avoid damage. 40 18 18 18 18 Anneltha God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Akara 		Nature Darkbloom: This creature's base stats are 20, plus 5 for each non-Ethereal Spell Gem it has equipped. The base stat that is increased by this trait is determined by the class of the Spell Gem. Only the first 8 Spell Gems are considered. 42 17 17 16 16 Ariamaki God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Adaxial 		Death Tools of Creation: At the start of battle, you gain access to 3 random perks. A description of these perks can be found in your battle history. This trait activates immediately before all other start-of-battle effects. This trait does not stack. 40 15 18 18 18 Alexandria God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Grom’met 		Sorcery Echobreather: This creature deals 20% more damage with attacks and spells, and takes 10% less damage for each different creature class fighting on your side. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 T'Mere M'rgo God Shop (Favor Rank 20) + Project 64

Colg 		Chaos What's Yours Is Mine: At the start of battle, your creatures gain a random enemy's trait. This trait does not stack. 30 18 18 18 18 Defeat Caliban at the Gate of the Gods + Project 591

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Godspawn: 

If all the creatures in your party belong to the  Godspawn race, at the start of battle, your Godspawn creatures gain their respective  Avatar's trait.

Strategy[edit]

Every Godspawn is different, adaptable for different kind of team, but Noxpit is widely considered to be the worst creature in Siralim Ultimate.

Multitheism perk of Fanatic gives Godspawn a trait of their "parent", which is great value since you can sneak 2 avatar traits into your team now without being constrained by Mastery requirement.

name: Golem
content:
Golem is a species encountered in wandering groups in Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Magma Golem 		Chaos Cataclysm: After this creature is Attacked, it afflicts enemies with Burning. If enemies already have this debuff, its potency is increased by 30% instead. 40 18 18 18 18 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 30

Regal Golem 		Life Sense of Purpose: At the start of this creature's turn, your creatures recover 35% Health. 42 17 17 17 17 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 45

Moss Golem 		Nature Mass Entanglement: After this creature Defends, each enemy has a 50% chance to be afflicted with Snared. 40 18 18 18 18 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 60

Gold Golem 		Life Chip Off the Block: After this creature is Attacked, your other creatures gain Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed equal to 15% of this creature's stats. 42 17 17 17 17 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 75

Haunted Golem 		Death Mechanical Illusion: After this creature is killed, it reveals the creature that was haunting it. 42 17 17 17 17 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 90

Nightmare Golem 		Death Bad Dreams: Each enemy has a 50% chance to start battles with Sleeping. 40 18 18 18 18 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 105

War Golem 		Chaos Triple Take: This creature Attacks and Casts spells 2 additional times. 40 17 17 17 17 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 120

Mud Golem 		Nature Slop: After this creature is Attacked, enemies are afflicted with Blind. If enemies already have this debuff, it decreases their Speed by 20% instead. 42 17 17 17 17 Frostbite Caverns, Temple of Lies, Forbidden Depths 135

Pumking 		Sorcery Pumpkin Spice: At the start of battle, your creatures swap their Maximum Health with their Defense. 40 18 18 18 18 Chaos Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Golem: 

At the start of battle, your  Golems gain the Triple Take trait. If they already have this trait, they gain the Chip Off The Block trait instead.

Mana for Treasure Golem cannot be obtained. When encountering the Treasure Golem, you get mana for Nugget.

Strategy[edit]

Iron Golem's trait is extremely powerful, as it shares already juicy traits of other golems with each other. This won't share traits if you change other creatures' race to  golem (via cockatrice or tribalist) - traits are shared before races are changed. If you can goad enemies into attacking you (with Sabotage or otherwise), Gold Golem's trait will soon make your team impenetrable.

War Golem's trait is awesome both in all-golem team and in fusions - more actions, with no damage reduction, no strings attached!

Maximum Health grows much faster with levels than Defense, so Pumpking can become nearly invulnerable, or very lethal in hands of a Siegemaster.

Trivia[edit]

Golem is a creature from Jewish mysticism and folklore; it is a person made from inanimate matter, usually via use of divine names.

name: Inquisitor
content:
This page is about a species. For other uses, see Inquisitor_(specialization).

 Inquisitors are a race encountered in wandering groups in Eternity's End and the Torture Chamber.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Carnivorous Inquisitor 		Nature Permanence: Your creatures' minions have a 50% lower chance to go away. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 20 15 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 37

Inquisitor Egglayer 		Nature Mass Summoning: At the start of battle, your creatures gain 1 to 3 random minions. This trait does not stack. 36 16 21 21 16 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 37

Watchful Inquisitor 		Nature The Walking Dead: Your creatures' Zombies minions now deal damage based on their master's highest stat. In addition, your creatures start battles with 1 Zombies. This trait does not stack. 36 16 21 21 16 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 37

Inquisitor Weaver 		Nature Pleasant Disease: When your creatures' Writhelings minions afflict a debuff, they also grant one of your creatures a random buff. In addition, your creatures start battles with 1 Writhelings. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 22 15 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 45

Infernal Inquisitor 		Nature Open The Portals: After this creature Attacks, it gains a random minion. After your other creatures Attack, they have a 30% chance to gain a random minion. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 20 15 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 60

Inquisitor Countess 		Nature On A Pale Horse: Your creatures' Conquest, Death, Famine, and War are 100% more powerful. This trait does not stack. 33 13 21 21 16 Torture Chamber, Eternity's End 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Inquisitor: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Inquisitors, they gain a random minion at the start of their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Inquisitors are tailored for a by-the-books  Necromancer build, relying completely on Minions for everything. However, a by-the-books Necromancer is slow by nature; for a faster build, look to a  Hound or  Lich team. Also, Carnivorous Inquisitor becomes obsolete after earning enough Perk Points to max out Midnight Bargain.

On a Master of Inquisitors team, the largest damage output will almost always be indirect damage. Whether raising your creatures' Defense to enhance their Zombies (as the trait The Walking Dead ignores Health), or raising their Health to enhance their Spiderlings, there's one trait your team will feel incomplete without: Synthesis. Have fun hunting for Discarded Blood if you haven't found any already!

Like many other builds, Inquisitors benefit from Dryad Keeper, Dryad Proliferator, and Dryad Vindicator's innate traits. More specifically, Inquisitor Weaver benefits from Writhelings now being able to cause rapid stat gains and losses with minimal effort on your creatures' part. Note that Writhelings have a maximum of 5 stacks, but Nox Sinon's innate trait raises that cap by 3.

name: Leech
content:
Leeches are a race encountered in wandering groups in the Arachnid Nest and the Titan's Wound. Unsurprisingly, these creatures are all about enhancing their Leeching buff.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Oculum Leech 		Death Bloodlust: Your creatures always have Leeching and receive 30% more healing. 40 23 18 18 13 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 10

Oozing Leech 		Death Blood Barrier: After your creatures are healed by their Leeching buff, they gain a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 50% of the amount recovered. This trait does not stack. 42 22 17 17 12 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 15

Leech Digger 		Death Cleansed Blood: Your creatures with Leeching deal 30% more damage and no longer take damage from Blighted. This trait does not stack. 43 23 17 17 12 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 30

Leech Devourer 		Death Coming Undone: After your creatures with Leeching Attack, they gain 10% Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. This trait does not stack. 40 23 18 18 13 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 45

Toxic Leech 		Death Imbibe: After your creatures with Leeching take damage, they are healed for an amount equal to 40% of the damage taken. This trait does not stack. 40 23 18 18 13 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 60

Moon Leech 		Death Deeper Sucking: Your creatures' Leeching buff is 100% more effective. This trait does not stack. 42 22 17 17 12 Arachnid Nest, Titan's Wound 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Leeche: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Leeches, their Leeching buff lasts forever and heals for 300% more. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

An all-Leech team makes for a weird  Cleric build, a vaguely unsatisfying  Bloodmage build, or a phenomenal  Paladin build. As Leeching works with sourced indirect damage, including the innate traits of Koloss Doombringer, Koloss Champion, and Koloss Eradicator in the build will turn the Retribution perk into an infinite Health scheme! Throw in Preta Cherub's newly-fixed Sundered and Undone trait to double-dip on damage reflection. However, this team does have an Achilles' Heel: unsourced damage from Minions and Debuffs.

Trivia[edit]

Liches, from old german for "corpse", are fantasy creatures that are usually really powerful necromancers that achieved immortality.

content:
