Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Manticore: 

Enemies have 5% less Attack and your creatures have 15% more Attack and Defense for each  Manticore fighting on your side. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Hell Knight is sure to love Prowling Manticore's trait.

Trivia[edit]

Manticore is a legendary creature from ancient Persian mythology: a lion with a human head and a scorpion's tail. "Manticore" is a greek spelling, while "Martyaxwar" is closer to the persian, meaning "man-eater".

name: Marionette
content:
Marionette is a species encountered Where the Dead Ships Dwell in wandering groups.


The realm depth at which each  Marionette first appears is currently unknown, and the values in the table below are a placeholder.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Marionette Medium 		Chaos Heartstrings: At the end of each enemy's turn, they Cast one of their spells. After an enemy with Cursed Casts a spell, they take damage equal 20% of this creature's Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 35 15 25 20 15 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Harlequin 		Chaos Circus Reverie: Your creatures deal additional damage with attacks and spells equal to 10% of their Attack for each buff their target has. This trait does not stack. 30 25 20 15 20 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Charlatan 		Chaos Hoodwink: Enemies always have Taunting. Enemies with Blind have a 50% chance to target Provoking allies with attacks and spells. This trait does not stack. 32 25 20 15 18 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Evangelist 		Chaos Adoration: At the end of each enemy's turn, they Cast a healing Spell Gem equipped by this creature in your favor. This trait does not stack. 35 15 25 20 15 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Virtuoso 		Chaos Glissando: Enemies always have Agile. The first time an enemy dodges each turn, each of your creatures Attack that enemy for 50% normal damage. This trait does not stack. 32 20 25 15 18 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Primadonna 		Chaos Spotlight: At the end of each enemy's turn, they Attack this creature for 15% normal damage and are afflicted with Cursed. This trait does not stack. 32 25 20 15 18 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Demoiselle 		Chaos Innocence: Enemies with Cursed take 10% more damage from this debuff for each time an enemy has taken Cursed damage in the current battle. This trait does not stack. 35 20 25 20 20 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Ballerina 		Chaos Grand Pas: Your creatures' attacks and spells ignore 20% of Defending and Provoking enemies' Defense. This effect is increased by 100% if the target is both Defending and Provoking. This trait does not stack. 30 20 20 15 25 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Marionette Hypnotist 		Chaos Entrancing: Enemies always have Defensive. After an enemy Defends, this creature afflicts them with Blind and Sleeping. This trait does not stack. 32 15 28 15 20 Where the Dead Ships Dwell 1

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Marionette: 

After your  Marionette creatures perform an action, the enemy in their corresponding position performs the same action and is afflicted with a random debuff. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

 Marionettes were contributed alongside the Mesmerist specialization by the backer "Hermit".

name: Mimic (species)
content:
Mimic is a very rare species.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Mimic 		Chaos Surprise!: This creature always starts battle at the top of the Timeline. 30 24 23 23 8 Rare Encounter from Treasure Chests 1

Pandora 		Chaos Pandora's Box: At the start of battle, this creature Casts 5 random spells, one from each class. 22 22 22 22 22 Rare Encounter from Treasure Chests 1

Killer Cooler 		Chaos Hydration: At the start of battle, this creature gains 5 random traits. 30 23 23 23 9 Chaos Guild 415

Gift Mimic 		Chaos Nothing But Coal: When this creature dies, it deals damage to enemies equal to 35% of its Attack. 30 21 24 24 7 Sorcery Guild 415


Strategy[edit]

Original Creature Mimic and/or its trait is indispensable, if you can't utilize no other ways to certainly start battles on the top of Timeline.

The Another rarely encountable creature Pandora is very unstable but has great stats and trait can cast 5 random spells like barrage as SoB effect.

Trivia[edit]

Mimics are living treasure chests from Dungeons&Dragons that became popular in other modern fantasy works.

This creature cannot Cast spells. 32 28 15 15 20 Cutthroat Jungle 1

The  Mireling race was contributed by the backer "Playmikesux".

name: Phoenix
content:
Phoenix is a species encountered in wandering groups in The Barrens and Land of Breath & Balance.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Thunderstruck Phoenix 		Sorcery Thundershock: After your creatures are resurrected, 3 enemies are afflicted with Stone. 28 21 21 11 21 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Royal Phoenix 		Sorcery Second Chance: When your creatures are resurrected, they have a 50% chance to be placed at the top of the Timeline. This trait does not stack. 31 21 21 11 21 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Volatile Phoenix 		Sorcery Buffet: After your creatures are resurrected, enemies take damage equal to 50% of this creature's Speed. 31 21 21 11 21 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Corrupted Phoenix 		Sorcery Final Breath: After an enemy is resurrected, it has a 75% chance to be killed again. 29 22 22 12 22 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 60

Ancestral Phoenix 		Sorcery Lifebinder: After your creatures kill an ally, your creatures are resurrected with 50% Health. 29 19 22 12 22 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 75

Transcended Phoenix 		Sorcery Alternate Reality: Your other creatures take 60% less damage while this creature is alive. Your other creatures take 40% more damage while this creature is dead. This trait does not stack. 32 22 21 11 21 The Barrens, Land of Breath & Balance 90

Divine Phoenix 		Life Terminus: At the start of this creature's turn, it is killed. Then, it is resurrected with 100% Health. 37 18 18 18 18 Life Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Phoenix: 

At the start of your  Phoenixes' turns, their on-resurrect effects have a 50% chance to activate. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

One of the best race on the builds focusing on-resurrect effects．Royal Phoenix can solve Timeline placement problems after resurrected with 50％ chance, Volatile Phoenix deals indirect damage to enemies, and Ancestral Phoenix supports party attack including being forced by enemy, etc．

Trivia[edit]

Phoenix is a legendary immortal bird that cyclically regenerates or is otherwise born again, from Greek, Egyptian, Persian mythology.

If you're not in a Realm, this trait acts as if you've collected 10 objects. This trait does not stack. 32 22 22 11 21 The Swamplands, Gambler's Hive 90

Trivia[edit]

Likely based on creatures from german folklore called Bilwis. Bilwis is described as a spirit that haunts corn-fields and wears a triangular hat.

Aen Rift Dancer 		Life Psychic Dance: All effects that activate when a creature Dodges will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 38 15 15 15 25 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Inaer Rift Dancer 		Life Fan Dance: All effects that activate when a creature Defends will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Nadin Rift Dancer 		Life Dark Dance: All effects that activate when a creature dies will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 46

Ramun Rift Dancer 		Life Sword Dance: All effects that activate when a creature deals critical damage will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 60

Apollinaire 		Life Renaissance: All effects that activate when a creature resurrects will activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack. 31 16 16 16 26 The Barrens, Sanctum Umbra, Land of Breath & Balance 75

Nax Rift Dancer 		Life What Is Left: This creature deals additional damage with attacks and spells equal to 1% of the stats it has gained in the current battle. 33 20 20 15 20 Life Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Rift Dancer: 

If all the creatures in your party belong to the  Rift Dancer race, their innate traits activate 1 additional time. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Rift Dancers are good supportive creatures with high Speed. Nearly any team can find a place for at least one Rift Dancer trait due to their broad utility.

An all-Rift Dancers team using their Mastery trait can perform shockingly effective combo shennanigans, especially as a  Doombringer, the specialization whose entire deal is "combo shennanigans." Their innate traits’ additional activation can bring your enemies to their doom if they can't stop you from dancing.

When an effect deals damage based on a creature's current Health, and the effect is repeated during one proc, the damage dealt will not decrease after each repeat deals damage. Paired with Apollinaire, Troll Youngster's innate trait can kill an enemy the instant it revives. When paired with Aerum Rift Dancer while Master of Rift Dancers is active, Whiptail Clutcher's trait deals damage equal to 90% of the current health of an enemy unfortunate enough to dodge an attack; this can be exploited to its full potential using Marionette Virtuoso's trait.

Note that damage from the traits of Whiptail Clutcher, Troll Youngster and many similar creatures is indirect damage, and is therefore reduced by Defense (albeit at a much lower rate than Attack or Spell damage), and traits such as Phase Knight's Endurance Aura.

name: Satyr
content:
Satyrs are a race encountered by playing flutes found within the Blood Grove.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Exiled Satyr 		Nature Languid Limbs: Enemies can only Attack once at a time. 29 17 22 17 17 Blood Grove (Pipes) 1

Spry Satyr 		Nature Somber Spirits: Enemies can only Cast spells once at a time. 29 14 22 17 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 15

Satyr Rammer 		Nature Meaningless Mind: Enemies can only gain each stat 1 time per turn. 33 17 22 12 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 30

Satyr Dominator 		Nature Rough Recovery: Enemies can only heal each other 1 time per turn. 32 16 21 16 21 Blood Grove (Pipes) 45

Satyr Squire 		Nature Faulty Fortune: Enemies can only gain 1 buff per turn. 32 17 22 11 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 60

Satyr Guardian 		Nature Scant Stamina: Enemies can only resurrect 1 time per battle. 32 17 22 11 22 Blood Grove (Pipes) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Satyr: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Satyrs, enemies start battles with 20% less stats. This trait does not stack.

Lore[edit]

Satyrs are the descendants of the Blood Grove's first colonists, humans from Vanelin transformed by consuming the Realm's fruit and water. They build settlements in the Grove's treetops, where they experience fewer  Eft attacks, and trade with the underground  Inquisitors through tree trunks hollowed out all the way down to their roots. The first colonists' warlike traditions, tempered by endless Eft predation, has made the Satyrs expert strategists.

Strategy[edit]

Including a Satyr trait or two can help a team cope with the consequences of high Realm Instability. A combo including Satyr Guardian's Scant Stamina and either Corrupted Phoenix's Final Breath or Troll Youngster's Rude Awakening generally speeds up battles, especially ones with many Life creatures, and may even clothesline certain bosses. To further restrict the enemy's other actions, consider the traits of  Tremors,  Vortexes, and  Wyverns.

Trivia[edit]

The Lore entries of Satyr Squire and Satyr Guardian mention a creature named Unleashed Satyr.

name: Sea Shambler
content:
Sea Shamblers are a race encountered in wandering groups Where the Dead Ships Dwell and on Faraway Enclave. These terrible sea undead make their enemies' Blighted Debuff deadlier until both sides of the battlefield are equally rotten.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Innocent Shambler 		Death Appetite For Eschar: After your creatures deal damage as a result of Blighted, they recover Health equal to 100% of the damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 32 22 22 17 22 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 40

Shambler Recruiter 		Death Chronic Affliction: After an enemy takes damage from Blighted, it has a 50% chance to take the same amount of damage again. This trait does not stack. 30 22 22 17 12 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 40

Shambler Rescuer 		Death Retrograde: Enemies with Blighted receive 50% more healing from all sources. This trait does not stack. 30 22 22 17 12 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 45

Lugubrious Shambler 		Death Aggravated Illness: Enemies take 50% more damage from Blighted. This trait does not stack. 33 23 23 17 12 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 60

Shambler Juggernaut 		Death Vicar's Blessing: After an enemy takes damage from Blighted, this creature has a 35% chance to Cast Minor Healing on it. This trait does not stack. 26 23 23 18 13 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 75

Shambler Benefactor 		Death You Monsters Are People: Enemies take 25% more damage from Blighted for each debuff they have. This trait does not stack. 32 19 22 18 14 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Sea Shambler: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Sea Shamblers, they take 12% less damage for each enemy that is afflicted with Blighted. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

The  Shadowbringer specialization appreciates the boosts Sea Shamblers give to damage from Blighted. Shamblers like Shadowbringers just as much, as the perk Touch of Darkness averts the question of where to put healing spells on a Debuff-focused, likely Death-Class team. Sea Shamblers aren't just for Shadowbringers, though. For example, a  Defiler can use Shambler Benefactor's trait to rub salt in foes' wounds.

Trivia[edit]

Globsters are aquatic animals decayed beyond recognition, known for inspiring countless stories of monsters such as sea serpents, modern-day plesiosaurs, and unkillable reptiles.

If anything to make build all Shade team rather than accept a beneficial one, and using conjunction with the Artifact's trait; Shackler's Domination associating Shackler's Chains can share Dungeon Shade's and Gateway Shade's trait with your other Shades. This means nearly Invincible like Shackler itself while your Shades are maintaining their Invisible buff.

name: Smith
content:
Smith is a species purchased from Azural in the Frostbite Caverns. They are the male counterpart to  Gorgons.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Brim Smith 		Sorcery Dissection: When your creatures Attack, they deal additional damage equal to 50% of the total Attack bonus on this creature's artifact. This trait does not stack. 36 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 1) 4

Crystal Smith 		Sorcery Fool's Axiom: Your creatures have additional Defense equal to 50% of the total Defense bonus on this creature's artifact. 34 19 17 22 17 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 5) 4

Dark Brim Smith 		Sorcery Battle Born: Your creatures' Spell Slots have a 10% greater chance to activate. 36 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 8) 4

Black Crystal Smith 		Sorcery Unbreakable: Your creatures' '[Class] Strength' Artifact properties prevent 50% more damage from that class. This trait does not stack. 33 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 11) 4

Depravity Smith 		Sorcery Braze: Your creatures' '[Class] Strength' Artifact properties deal 50% more damage to that class. This trait does not stack. 36 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 14) 4

Pestilence Crafter 		Sorcery Incursion: Your creatures' 'Afflict debuff On Damage' properties have a 100% greater chance to activate. This trait does not stack. 37 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 18) 4

Hammer Lord 		Sorcery War Forged: Your creatures' 'Gain buff On Damage' properties have a 100% greater chance to activate. This trait does not stack. 34 21 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 25) 4

Disease Crafter 		Sorcery Imbue: Your creatures' Stat Slots provide 15% more benefit. This trait does not stack. 37 22 17 17 17 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 28) 4

Death Crafter 		Sorcery Honed Blades: Your creatures share 15% of their Artifacts' bonuses from Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed with each other. This trait does not stack. 34 19 16 21 16 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 32) 4

War Crafter 		Sorcery Warcraft: This creature's Stat Slots provide 40% more benefit. 35 20 15 20 15 Azural God Shop (Favor Rank 35) 4

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Smith: 

At the start of battle, if all the creatures in your party are  Smiths, they share their Artifact's trait with the creature to the left.

Strategy[edit]

  •  Gladiator's Elbow Grease stacks additively with the effects of War Crafter and Disease Crafter, resulting in a total bonus to Artifact stat slots of 90% and 65%, respectively.
  • 'Stat Slots' refers to the stat bonuses an Artifact gives, including the innate stat bonus from the Artifact (e.g. the Intelligence bonus from Staves), but not bonuses from Nether Stones.
  • Death Crafter/Honed Blades:
    • Shares all bonuses to stats an Artifact grants, including innate bonuses, stat slots, and bonuses from Nether Stones.
    • Stacks with  Gladiator's Elbow Grease, Nether Imbuement, and Yseros' The Truth.
  •  Trickster's Whetbone and Prey perks play nicely with the Brim Smith's Dissection trait. Make it even stronger with:
    • Ferro's rank 40 perk will make the bearer's Proficient buff 100% more effective
    • The Mathematics Textbook will boost your Artifact's first stat slot by 400% (so make sure it is Attack)

name: Sparktail
content:
Sparktails are a race found in wandering groups Where the Dead Ships Dwell and at the Faraway Enclave. Damaging these electric fish was many creatures' last mistake.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Sparktail Courier 		Sorcery Recompense: After this creature is Attacked, it deals damage to all enemies equal to 30% of the damage taken. 39 19 19 17 17 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 40

Sparktail Officer 		Sorcery Ravish: After this creature takes damage, it deals damage to the enemy equal to 100% of the damage taken. 38 18 21 16 16 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 40

Sparktail Student 		Sorcery Bombshell: Your creatures deal 75% more damage when it is not their turn. This trait does not stack. 38 21 21 16 16 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 45

Sparktail Searcher 		Sorcery Conviviality: This creature deals additional damage equal to 5% of the damage your creatures have taken in the current battle. 39 19 19 16 16 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 60

Sparktail Engineer 		Sorcery Comeuppance: After this creature takes damage from spells, it deals damage to all enemies equal to 30% of the damage taken. 38 21 21 16 16 Faraway Enclave, Where the Dead Ships Dwell 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Sparktail: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Sparktails, they share their innate traits with each other.

Strategy[edit]

A Master of Sparktails team with all six fish can succeed regardless of level. With a powerful synergy that turns the enemy's stats against them, even the  Deprived can go far with Sparktails. Most of the team's worries will be the cases where enemies can't instantly kill themselves by damaging your creatures, such as due to the "chance to avoid damage" Realm Property or the traits of certain bosses.

According to The Great Big List, Sparktail Professor's Seeth only boosts damage dealt by other Sparktail traits.

name: Unicorn
content:
This species is encountered in Azure Dream by wishing on Stars for glory. Its members are Unicorn Vivifier, Unicorn Consecrator, Pegasus, Unicorn Thundercracker, Unicorn Stormbringer, Unicorn Firewalker, Unicorn Holycaster.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Unicorn Vivifier 		Life Reinvigoration: After this creature Attacks, it resurrects a random ally with 50% Health. 31 21 16 17 22 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars), Tutorial Starter 1

Unicorn Consecrator 		Life Circle of Life: After this creature Attacks, it heals allies for 50% of the damage dealt. 33 23 15 15 20 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 25

Unicorn Stormbringer 		Life Righteous Winds: After this creature Attacks, it increases a random ally's Attack and Intelligence by 50%. 30 21 16 16 21 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 30

Unicorn Holycaster 		Life Divine Mending: After this creature Attacks, it heals its ally with the lowest Health for 200% of the damage dealt. 32 22 17 14 19 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 45

Pegasus 		Life Chrysaor's Ambition: After this creature Attacks, it gives a random ally 2 random buffs. 36 16 16 21 16 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 60

Unicorn Thundercracker 		Life Spur of the Heavens: After this creature Attacks, it has a 50% chance to move a random ally to the top of the Timeline. 31 21 17 14 22 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 75

Unicorn Firewalker 		Life Eruption: After this creature Attacks, it has a 50% chance to Cast Volcano. 30 21 16 16 21 Azure Dream (Wishing Stars) 90

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Unicorn: 

Your  Unicorns Attack 2 additional times. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Unicorns are a species with high Attack and Speed, have very good on-attack procs, and therefore make for good attackers. Unicorn Vivifier can be used on many team comps for survivability or to easily trigger on-resurrection procs. A good example would be fusing it with Nix Shadowjumper. (expand this one)

Trivia[edit]

Unicorn is a mythical creature from antiquity, found in Indus Valley art and greek works of natural history. Many thought it to be a real animal.

Pegasus is a mythical winged horse-god from Greek mythology, sired by Poseidon.

name: Uralos
content:
Uralos species is encountered in The Swamplands and Amalgam Gardens in wandering groups.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Uralos Knight 		Nature Formation: After your Uralos creatures take damage from an attack or spell, your  Uralos creatures gain 5% Defense. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 16

Uralos Spellslinger 		Nature Escalation: After your  Uralos creatures manually Cast a spell, your  Uralos creatures gain 35% Intelligence. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 16

Uralos Spearmaster 		Nature War Dance: After your  Uralos creatures Defend or Provoke, your  Uralos creatures gain 20% Speed. This trait does not stack. 40 18 18 18 18 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 30

Uralos Savage 		Nature Hunger for Blood: After your Uralos creatures Attack, your  Uralos creatures gain 10% Attack. 40 18 18 18 18 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 45

Uralos Trickster 		Nature Cull the Weak: After an enemy is killed, your  Uralos creatures gain 15% stats. 40 15 18 18 18 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 60

Uralos Healer 		Nature Healing Aura: At the start of your  Uralos creatures' turns, they recover Health equal to 25% of their highest stat. This trait does not stack. 42 17 17 17 17 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 75

Uralos Warchief 		Nature Commanding Presence: Your  Uralos creatures' innate traits are 50% more powerful. This trait does not stack. 38 25 15 20 22 The Swamplands, Amalgam Gardens 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Uralos: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Uraloses, they have a 50% chance to avoid indirect damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Uralos traits depend on others in their party also being of that species, so you should either use a team of Uraloses or change the species of others via Clawing Cockatrice's trait, Tribalist perk or, worst come to worst, Epiphany spell. Uralos gain stats when they act, and can escalate greatly if a battle isn't finished quickly. I recommend taking traits that interact with stat gain (Doomsday Spectre) and make some actions automatic (for example, fusing Spearmaster with Valkyrie Queen for up to 6 guaranteed triggers of War Dance).

If you have unlocked Gate of the Gods, Avatar of Meraxis will allow Uralos to grow without any limit in prolonged battles.

name: Voidlord
content:
Voidlord is a species encountered by interacting with Cages in Bastion of the Void.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Angelic Voidlord 		Death Shadow Bulwark: This creature always has Repelling, and takes 90% less indirect damage. 32 12 15 25 20 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 52

Imperial Voidlord 		Death Contortion: After your creatures with Repelling take damage from attacks or spells, they reflect 50% of the damage taken back on the enemy. This trait does not stack. 37 17 17 22 17 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 52

Feral Voidlord 		Death Esoteric Alteration: Your creatures' Repelling buffs now have a 60% chance to prevent damage from spells. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 21 16 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 52

Demonic Voidlord 		Death Aether Eyes: Your creatures' Repelling buffs now have a 35% chance to ignore damage from attacks. This trait does not stack. 33 13 13 26 21 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 60

Vampiric Voidlord 		Death Mind Fortress: After your creatures take damage from a spell, they gain Repelling. 36 16 16 21 21 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 64

Subversive Voidlord 		Death Return to Source: Your creatures' Repelling buffs now give them a 50% chance to avoid being afflicted by debuffs. This trait does not stack. 36 16 16 21 16 Bastion of the Void (Cages) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Voidlord: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Voidlords, they always have Repelling and take 30% less damage. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Lore[edit]

These creatures can destroy anything they touch, leaving not even atoms. Dragan, a Raven from the Refuge of the Magi, theorizes that those things come from outside the material universe, same as Watchers. After his ambitious experiment to create Laglor was botched, Dragan disappeared and the number of Voidlords started to grow exponentially, and it took efforts of Tenebris and Mortem to create the Bastion of the Void and contain them.

Strategy[edit]

Vulpes are a bit of a gamble, but getting your spells off before the battle properly starts can be huge.

Evoker happens to know how to equip creatures with gems of other classes; otherwise, you'd need to spend enchantment slots on changing the gems' classes, or use the Materium relic.

Wight is a species encountered in wandering groups.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Terror Wight 		Death Dark Embrace: After your creatures die, this creature gains 25% of their Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. 41 21 16 16 16 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 13

Gravebane Wight 		Death Woe: After this creature dies, enemies take damage equal to 30% of its Maximum Health. 41 21 18 18 18 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 15

Reclusive Wight 		Death Resurrection Code: After this creature dies, it has a 50% chance to resurrect all allies with 50% Health. 42 22 17 14 17 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 30

Roofstalker Wight 		Death Afterlife: After this creature dies for the first time in battle, it resurrects with 50% Health, and 50% more Attack, Intelligence, Defense, and Speed. 41 21 16 16 16 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 45

Dread Wight 		Death Undeath: After this creature dies, it has a 50% chance to resurrect with 100% Health. 37 17 18 18 18 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 60

Trepidation Wight 		Death Last Stand: After this creature dies, it Attacks random enemies 5 times. 37 22 17 17 17 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 75

Frostbite Wight 		Death Cold Like War: After an enemy is resurrected, this creature Attacks it 2 times and afflicts it with Frozen. 41 21 18 18 18 Path of the Damned, Kingdom of Heretics 90

Headless Wight 		Death No Head Necessary: While this creature is dead, it has a 50% chance to Attack enemies at the start of their turns. 38 18 18 18 18 Death Guild 415

Holy Wight 		Life Praise the Light: After your creatures are resurrected, they Cast Holy Nova. This trait does not stack. 31 16 21 21 16 Life Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wight: 

Your  Wights always resurrect with 100% Health and Shelled.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Wight is an old germanic word meaning, generically, "thing" or "creature"; it became associated with undead in modern fantasy.

buffs.name: Shelled
buffs.description: Shelled negates the next incoming attack or spell.

debuffs.name: Frozen
debuffs.description: Frozen creatures cannot act until they thaw; chance to thaw increases each turn.

name: Wisp
content:
Wisp is a species encountered by interacting with Ghosts in Bastion of the Void.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Dancing Wisp 		Life Overpowered: This creature deals 100% more damage. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 36 16 16 16 26 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Abyssal Wisp 		Life Lyra: This creature Attacks and Casts spells 3 additional times. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 35 15 15 15 25 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Laughing Wisp 		Life Ethereal: This creature is immune to debuffs and takes 50% less damage. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 36 16 16 16 26 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Scheming Wisp 		Life Poof!: This creature always deals critical damage and has an independent 90% chance to Dodge attacks. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 35 15 15 18 25 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 52

Wisp Watcher 		Life Spasm: At the start of this creature's turn, it Casts its first five non-Ethereal Spell Gems. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 36 16 16 16 26 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 60

Wisp Seer 		Life The Virtue of Patience: At the start of this creature's turn, your creatures gain 3 random buffs. After this creature takes damage, it is sent to the bottom of the Timeline. 37 17 17 15 25 Bastion of the Void (Ghosts) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wisp: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Wisps, they have a 25% chance to avoid damage and gain Shelled at the start of their turns. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Wisps' traits have both amazing strength and alarming weakness. To work safely, take a little creativities; for some examples, fusing Mimic, cast Timewalk or Wormhole, or use Reaver's Sure-footed perk to negate forcing move.

buffs.description: Shelled negates the next incoming attack or spell.

debuffs.description: Weak creatures deal 50% less damage.

name: Wolpertinger
content:
Wolpertinger is a species encountered in Amalgam Gardens by staring into fire of Neon Braziers.

Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Docile Wolpertinger 		Nature Ditto: After one of your creatures manually Casts a Scourge spell, this creature Casts the same Scourge spell. 28 18 21 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vile Wolpertinger 		Nature Fraud: Your creatures' Scourge spells no longer consume Charges and cannot be Sealed. 29 19 19 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Volatile Wolpertinger 		Nature Prey: At the start of battle, your creatures gain a Scourge spell that matches their class. This trait does not stack. 30 20 20 15 20 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Viridian Wolpertinger 		Nature Bottom Feeder: Your creatures' Scourge spells also afflict the target with a debuff that is based on the type of Scourge spell that is Cast. This trait does not stack. 27 20 20 15 20 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Volcanic Wolpertinger 		Nature Whimsical: Your creatures' Scourge spells deal 100% more damage. This trait does not stack. 28 18 18 16 21 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vampiric Wolpertinger 		Nature Soul Sucker: After your creatures Cast a damaging spell, they recover Health equal to 30% of the damage dealt. This trait does not stack. 29 19 19 16 19 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vicious Wolpertinger 		Nature Colorblind: After this creature Attacks, it Casts a Scourge spell that matches the target's class. 33 23 18 13 18 Amalgam Gardens (Braziers) 64

Vernal Wolpertinger 		Nature Camaraderie: After this creature Defends, it gains a Barrier that absorbs damage equal to 100% of its Maximum Health. If this creature already has a Barrier, it gives it to a random ally instead. 31 21 21 15 20 Nature Guild 415

Villous Wolpertinger 		Nature Echoing Incantation: This creatures' single-target spells have a 35% chance to be Cast 1 additional time. This effect can occur repeatedly. 35 15 25 10 20 Nature Guild 415

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wolpertinger: 

If all the creatures in your party are  Wolpertingers, their Scourge spells always deal critical damage and have a 50% chance to be Cast 1 additional time. This effect can occur multiple times in a row. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

Trivia[edit]

Wolpertinger is a cryptid from the alpine forests of Bavaria and Baden-Württemberg in Southern Germany.

buffs.name: Barrier
buffs.description: Barrier absorbs a certain amount of damage. After the maximum amount of damage is dealt, the buff is removed.

name: Wyvern
content:
Wyvern is a species encountered by interacting with Eggs in Faraway Enclave.


Creature Class Trait Source Realm Depth

Wyvern Windrider 		Chaos Spell Stealer: After an enemy Casts a spell on all its allies, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the same spell on your creatures as well. This trait does not stack. 37 16 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Skystalker 		Chaos Deep Inhalation: After an enemy Casts a spell on all your creatures, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the spell on the enemy creatures as well. This trait does not stack. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Sonicscreecher 		Chaos Bounce: After an enemy Casts a single-target spell on one of your creatures, this creature has a 50% chance to Cast the spell on a random enemy. This trait does not stack. 36 16 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 43

Wyvern Airslayer 		Chaos Wings of Despair: When an enemy would gain a buff, this creature has a 50% chance to gain it instead. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 45

Wyvern Daybreaker 		Chaos Fallen Star: When an enemy would gain stats, this creature has a 50% chance to gain them instead. 33 13 21 16 21 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 60

Wyvern Toxicfang 		Chaos Currents: After an enemy gains stats, this creature afflicts it with a random debuff. 35 15 20 15 20 Faraway Enclave (Eggs) 75

Mastery Trait, provided by the Sigil of the Wyvern: 

Your  Wyverns take 80% less damage from spells. This trait does not stack.

Strategy[edit]

name: Yeti
content:

Frostbite Yeti 		Sorcery Brain Freeze: After this creature is Attacked, the enemy is afflicted with Frozen. 33 18 16 21 16 Frostbite Caverns (Frozen Yetis) 45
